title: banksia longifolia var. pubescens
intro: Banksia longifolia var. pubescens is a variety of the Banksia longifolia tree, commonly known as the woolly-leaved banksia or woolly-headed banksia, characterized by its distinctive grey or brown, woolly leaves. Traditionally, the plant's leaves have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of Banksia longifolia var. pubescens have been used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The leaves can be prepared as a tea or infused oil for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.
